31.reason: [Use of 'and' keyword from iso646.h may not be supported in all C environments; standard C uses '&&'. Also, left-to-right order of leaf nodes is not guaranteed with this array-based complete binary tree simulation because unused internal nodes may break in-order traversal logic.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Use of 'and' keyword from iso646.h may not be supported in all C environments; standard C uses '&&'. Also, left-to-right order of leaf nodes is not guaranteed with this array-based complete binary tree simulation because unused internal nodes may break in-order traversal logic.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The use of 'and' is invalid in standard C without including <iso646.h> properly or using &&; however, <iso646.h> is included, so this may compile in some environments but is non-portable and can cause compilation errors in strict C compilers that don't recognize 'and' as a keyword even with the header. Additionally, the logical condition should use '&&' for clarity and portability.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [Using 'and' instead of '&&' may not be supported in all compilers without including <iso646.h>, but even with it, some environments might not recognize it correctly; however, the real issue is that the array-based tree can access out-of-bound indices when n is large, leading to undefined behavior. But more critically, the condition for leaf node fails when zero values are inserted because the tree uses zero to indicate empty nodes, so inserting 0 will be treated as an empty node.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The use of 'and' is invalid in standard C without including <iso646.h> properly or using &&; however, <iso646.h> is included, so this may compile in some environments but is non-portable and may cause compilation errors in strict C compilers that don't recognize 'and' as a keyword even with the header. Additionally, accessing tree[now<<1] and tree[now<<1|1] may go out of bounds if the array isn't large enough, but the main error causing compilation failure is likely the use of 'and'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [Using 'and' instead of '&&' may not be supported in all compilers without proper inclusion or flag; although <iso646.h> defines 'and', some environments might still have issues, but more critically, the logic fails to correctly identify leaf nodes when zero values are inserted because the tree uses zero-initialized memory to indicate empty nodes, so inserting a zero will be treated as an empty node.]
